text.title: LlamaIndex: Workflows and Multi-Agent Orchestration
text.summary: Event-driven Workflow architecture, step design, state management, and AgentWorkflow multi-agent patterns.

rule.statement: Define Pydantic Event models for all step inputs and outputs; use typed events — not raw dicts — to enforce the pipeline contract between steps.
rule.rationale: LlamaIndex Workflow step routing is based on event type matching. A typo in an event class name causes a silent no-op where the step never executes. Type-safe events surface this at definition time, not at runtime.

antipattern.description: Using string-keyed context dictionaries to pass data between all steps.
antipattern.reason: ctx.set/get with arbitrary string keys creates implicit coupling and makes step dependencies invisible. Define explicit Event fields for step outputs; use ctx only for values genuinely shared across multiple steps (e.g., original query).

rule.statement: Use ctx.collect_events() to wait for multiple parallel event types before proceeding; emit multiple events from a single step via self.send_event() to trigger parallel processing.
rule.rationale: LlamaIndex Workflow does not block on a single step — parallel branches run concurrently. collect_events() provides a typed barrier that waits for all required event types before the combining step executes.

rule.statement: Use AgentWorkflow with FunctionAgent agents for multi-agent patterns; define can_handoff_to explicitly on each agent so the orchestration graph is deterministic.
rule.rationale: AgentWorkflow pre-configures the event-driven handoff mechanism. Without explicit can_handoff_to, agents may hand off to any other agent including themselves, creating infinite loops. Named handoff targets make the graph auditable.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.agent.workflow import AgentWorkflow, FunctionAgent
researcher = FunctionAgent( name="researcher", description="Researches topics and finds relevant information", tools=[search_tool], llm=Settings.llm, system_prompt="You are a research specialist. Find accurate information. Be thorough.", can_handoff_to=["analyst", "writer"], ) workflow = AgentWorkflow( agents=[researcher, analyst, writer], initial_agent="researcher", ) result = await workflow.run(task="Research Q3 performance and write a summary")
